questions.question: State the basic principle of ANOVA and explain its significance in the context of designing experiments.
questions.hint: ANOVA stands for Analysis of Variance. It is a statistical technique used to compare means of two or more groups to identify any significant differences between them.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a one-way ANOVA and a two-way ANOVA? Provide a suitable example to illustrate your explanation.
questions.hint: One-way ANOVA is used to compare means of three or more groups, while two-way ANOVA is used to compare means of two factors (independent variables) and their interaction.

questions.question: Describe the advantages and limitations of using Latin-square designs in ANOVA.
questions.hint: Latin-square designs offer the advantage of reducing the effects of extraneous variables and improving the accuracy of results. However, the main limitation is that it can only accommodate three levels of each factor.
